definition:
1. to rub out , πῦρ ἐκτρ. to produce  fire by rubbing , (Xenophon Historicus):—; to rub hard , (Sophocles Tragicus) 
2. to rub out , i. e. to destroy root and branch , (Herdotus Historicus); βίον ἐκτρ. to bring  life to a wretched end , (Sophocles Tragicus):—;Pass., πρόρριζος ἐκτέτριπται (Herdotus Historicus) 
3. to rub constantly, wear out , (Euripides) (ML)
